text:
**IBEW Traveler Snapshot — Monday, July 6, 2026**

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** is running hot with 43 calls and 187 hands needed. The Meta Data Center in Lebanon is the crown jewel—Meade Electric is stacking **10% above scale** on top of a **$53.80 base**, running 5-12s Monday-Friday plus Saturday work. Industrial Electric's Lilly project offers rotating 7-10s with a strict 13-day rule. This is top-tier data center work with real money.

**LU-494 Milwaukee, WI** has 41 calls chasing 169 hands, and the Port Washington Data Center is paying **JW scale plus $10/hr** across four contractors. It's a repost, which means steady turnover—good for newer travelers looking to build book time fast.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** is converting travelers into locals with 75 calls total at the Polaris Forge Data Center. Parsons Electric is sweetening the deal with **$100/day non-taxed per diem** Monday-Friday, bumping to **$150 weekends**, plus shift differentials hitting **31.4%** on nights. The **$44.80 scale** climbs fast with incentives.

**LU-683 Columbus, OH** is solid mid-market work at **$73.42 scale**—the highest advertised rate on today's board. Mid City dominates with 25 calls across various locations. ESI's Cologix Data Center gig pays double-time OT with a 40-hour M-F minimum.

**LU-613 Atlanta, GA** shows 36 calls with **$48 top scale**. Inglett & Stubbs' QTS Fayette project and Wellstar Hospital work offer reliable 4-10 schedules at **$40 base**, ideal for builders wanting steady paychecks over prestige.

Data center work is eating the board—706 calls across 87 locals means demand is real, but it's concentrated in infrastructure megaprojects.
